summary: Iteration 5 — UI-only bug fix verification: 'Selected compounds: N / M' denominator removed. All 6 acceptance criteria PASS on preview URL. Backend untouched (skipped per request). Verified: (1) selection-count chip renders 'Selected compounds: <N>' with NO '/' character in ALL states — pre-search (0), post-search (0), after selecting 5 rows (5), and across cross-session persistence (5 still shown after switching to smaller Aloe vera dataset of 101 rows); (2) proceed-count contains ONLY the number, and its parent line reads '<N> compound(s) selected' with no '/' — regex verified on both singular/plural (though only plural 5 hit here, code path still uses selectedCount === 1 conditional); (3) cross-session persistence still functional — selected 5 on Withania (164 rows), navigated Home → back → searched Aloe vera (101 rows), both counters still correctly show 5 (proving localStorage['drSlash.selection.v1'] persistence is intact AND the misleading 5/101 or 5/164 ratio no longer appears); (4) selection/deselection updates BOTH counters synchronously in real-time (chip=5,proceed=5 → toggle off row 0 → chip=4,proceed=4); (5) proceed dialog description text unaffected — 'You have selected 5 compounds for downstream analysis…' renders correctly on Aloe vera context with cross-session-persisted selections; (6) no NEW console errors (only pre-existing benign '<span> cannot be a child of <option>' warning at page-size <select>, flagged in iterations 1–4). No new backend regressions expected — no backend changes made in this iteration.
critical_code_review_comments: Fix is minimal and correct: line 539 renders 'Selected compounds: {selectedCount}' (chip) and line 815 wraps selectedCount in <span data-testid=proceed-count> followed by ' compound{s} selected' — both denominator-free. Semantically clean.

Minor consideration (carry-over from iteration 4): setMany(compounds, false) in the 'Clear' button clears only currently-loaded compounds. If cross-session persistence is emphasized (as it now is by removing the /M denominator), consider offering an explicit 'Clear ALL persisted selections' action to prevent stale selections from earlier plants silently persisting invisibly to the user. Not blocking — user still sees the total count in both places.

The proceed-bar's caption 'Selections persist across search, filters and pages.' remains accurate and now aligns UX-wise with the new counter (previously the 'N/M' visual conflicted with this promise).
seed_data_creation: None — cleared localStorage['drSlash.selection.v1'] at test start; used pre-cached Withania somnifera (164 rows) and Aloe vera (101 rows) results.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 5 delta = 2-line UI fix removing the '/ {compounds.length}' denominator from (a) selection-count chip and (b) proceed-bar caption. Both counters now render only the raw selectedCount from useSelection() context. Cross-session persistence via localStorage['drSlash.selection.v1'] is unchanged and still functional. For any future work adjusting the selection UX, keep in mind that filtered-view selection (select-all header, 'Deselect N in view' button) still uses filtered array counts — those are different UX affordances and are correctly preserved. The confirm dialog description text (`You have selected {selectedCount} compound(s) for downstream analysis`) was also inspected and is denominator-free.
rca of the issue: Original bug: chip and proceed-bar rendered 'Selected compounds: <selectedCount> / <compounds.length>' where selectedCount is the persistent cross-search cumulative selection while compounds.length is the CURRENTLY loaded dataset. Because selections persist across plant searches (by design, via localStorage), selectedCount could exceed compounds.length after user searches a smaller plant (e.g. 215 selected earlier / now only 53 in current results → '215 / 53' — confusing and misleading). Fix: drop the ' / {compounds.length}' fragment from both render sites — the correct semantic is 'total selected across all searches', not a ratio to the current view. Verified: after selecting 5 in Withania (164 rows) then loading Aloe vera (101 rows), chip shows 'Selected compounds: 5' (not '5 / 101') and proceed line shows '5 compounds selected'.
